flagged_messages.message_text: ROW-783: 'You gonna pay or do I file contempt?' | ROW-789: 'You're in contempt at this point' | ROW-790: 'You need to borrow off someone or something otherwise you'll be going back to court' | ROW-824: 'You still haven't paid your half of daycare and swim which was due last Thursday. If I don't see payment soon I'll reach out to the lawyer. She's still on retainer' | ROW-828: 'This isn't pay when you feel like it. Your ordered by the court to pay within 7 days'
flagged_messages.primary_reasoning: FIRST contempt filing threat in dataset. ROW-783 'You gonna pay or do I file contempt?' is the first explicit threat to initiate formal legal contempt proceedings against Eli. ROW-789 escalates to 'You're in contempt at this point' (declaration of legal status). ROW-790 adds 'you'll be going back to court' (future legal action threat). ROW-824 introduces the lawyer-on-retainer disclosure: 'I'll reach out to the lawyer. She's still on retainer' — the 'still on retainer' element signals active legal infrastructure, not just a hypothetical threat. ROW-828 cites the court order ('ordered by the court to pay within 7 days'). ADD-002 temporal rule: first instance of contempt-filing category = Sev-2 (initially credible, fear-inducing). Per Eli's CP2 answer, legal threats were 'genuinely fear-inducing' initially. Both the contempt filing and lawyer-retainer disclosure are first instances. Context: Eli confirms he was late on child support/expense payments (genuine underlying obligation). The threats are deployed in an escalatory pattern across Nov 11-14 — two separate days with escalating legal language. The phrasing 'She's still on retainer' is particularly designed to convey organizational readiness for legal action. ADD-002: paper-tiger recognition develops over repeated non-follow-through; at this early stage (B-003, Nov 2022), these threats were credible.

peripheral_flags.note: ROW-820-821: 'Being a house bitch, taxi and working two jobs...' + 'stop being a lil bitch' — Abby-framework contempt attack (RF-04 primary; ADD-001: RF-05 peripheral aggressive-tone only). Not RF-05 primary.
ROW-1047: 'Not that I need to explain to your dumbass' — contemptuous intelligence attack (RF04-ADD-002 pattern). RF-04 primary.

flagged_messages.context_notes: Eli was late on child support and shared expense payments — the underlying obligation is real (genuine non-payment). The contempt threat is technically legitimate (she can file contempt). The RF-05 element is the aggressive deployment of legal threat language as compliance pressure during co-parenting exchanges, and the escalation from 'do I file' to 'you're already in contempt' in a single conversation.
flagged_messages.quote_excerpt: 'You gonna pay or do I file contempt?' (ROW-783); 'She's still on retainer' (ROW-824)

arcs_promoted_this_batch: ARC-RF05-001: CANDIDATE → WATCHING. Second distinct episode (B-002 = Halloween implied threat; B-003 = first contempt filing Sev-2). First Sev-2 signal. Threshold for ACTIVE = third distinct episode with primary signal.

batch_observations.tone_assessment: B-003 (Nov-Dec 2022) introduces the first contempt filing threat — the more serious legal-threat category that aligns with Eli's stated primary fear (lawfare). The threat language is direct and escalatory across two days, with the 'still on retainer' disclosure signaling active legal infrastructure.
batch_observations.cross_rf_context: RF-04 B-003 includes first predation framing instances (RF-04 CP3 items from B-003). The contempt threats here are distinct from the Abby-attack content and should be tracked on the RF-05 arc independently.
